{"id":"project-LICHEN-o34u","title":"edhoc: export_oscore callable before handshake completes, derives keys from zeroed state","description":"File: rust/lichen-oscore/src/edhoc.rs
Lines: 564-596 (EdhocInitiator::export_oscore), 929-959 (EdhocResponder::export_oscore)

EdhocInitiator::export_oscore() and EdhocResponder::export_oscore() can be called at any time, including before the handshake completes. If called prematurely:

For initiator (before process_message_2):
- prk_4e3m is still [0; 32] from InitiatorState::default()
- th_4 is still [0; 32]

For responder (before process_message_3):
- Same: prk_4e3m and th_4 are zero

The HKDF derivation will succeed (no error) but produce deterministic keys derived from all-zero material. These keys will NOT match the peer's keys, causing all subsequent OSCORE operations to fail with DecryptFailed.

This is different from:
- 9n1p (double export): calling export twice after handshake
- wf0s (use after export): reusing EDHOC after export

Fix: Add state tracking to enforce handshake completion before export. For example:
- Add a 'completed: bool' field to InitiatorState/ResponderState
- Set it true at end of process_message_2/process_message_3
- Check it in export_oscore, return EdhocError::InvalidState if false

Alternative: Check prk_4e3m != [0; 32] (weaker - relies on PRK not being zero by chance).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T17:15:12.615156Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-12T17:15:12.61518Z"}

{"id":"project-LICHEN-o34u","title":"edhoc: export_oscore callable before handshake completes, derives keys from zeroed state","description":"File: rust/lichen-oscore/src/edhoc.rs
Lines: 564-596 (EdhocInitiator::export_oscore), 929-959 (EdhocResponder::export_oscore)

EdhocInitiator::export_oscore() and EdhocResponder::export_oscore() can be called at any time, including before the handshake completes. If called prematurely:

For initiator (before process_message_2):
- prk_4e3m is still [0; 32] from InitiatorState::default()
- th_4 is still [0; 32]

For responder (before process_message_3):
- Same: prk_4e3m and th_4 are zero

The HKDF derivation will succeed (no error) but produce deterministic keys derived from all-zero material. These keys will NOT match the peer's keys, causing all subsequent OSCORE operations to fail with DecryptFailed.

This is different from:
- 9n1p (double export): calling export twice after handshake
- wf0s (use after export): reusing EDHOC after export

Fix: Add state tracking to enforce handshake completion before export. For example:
- Add a 'completed: bool' field to InitiatorState/ResponderState
- Set it true at end of process_message_2/process_message_3
- Check it in export_oscore, return EdhocError::InvalidState if false

Alternative: Check prk_4e3m != [0; 32] (weaker - relies on PRK not being zero by chance).","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"AI Agent","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T17:15:12.615156Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-22T20:09:20.106280758Z","started_at":"2026-07-22T20:09:20.106280758Z","lease_expires_at":"2026-07-22T20:14:20.106280758Z","heartbeat_at":"2026-07-22T20:09:20.106280758Z"}

new_value: Fixed with completed:bool in InitiatorState and ResponderState (default false, set true after successful process_message_2/3), guarded in export_oscore returning OscoreError::NoContext. Dedicated test_export_before_handshake_returns_error confirms behavior. Matches suggested fix (used NoContext instead of InvalidState for OSCORE layer). No code changes needed as already implemented. Commit: 6c9115b07
